sum: TI's first new 300mm wafer fab on its Sherman mega-site began production December 17, 2025 — three-and-a-half years after groundbreaking. The site is planned for four connected fabs totaling up to $40B in investment and up to 3,000 direct jobs. SM2 shell construction is already complete and interior equipping is underway.
cre: Multi-fab industrial mega-campus on a large land position in North Texas. Anchor tenant occupancy generating sustained demand for supplier-ecosystem and support-services space in the Sherman/Grayson County submarket. SM2 shell completion signals near-term additional absorption.
changeNote: Production start (SM1 first wafers) confirmed December 17, 2025 — converts project from construction phase to operational. SM2 exterior shell confirmed complete per TI June 2025 announcement; now entering equipment installation phase.

sum: Samsung Foundry completed its equipment move-in ceremony at Taylor Fab 1 in April 2026 and is targeting risk production in the second half of 2026. The company simultaneously announced an early feasibility review for a second Taylor fab focused on 2nm processes, driven by a $16.5B Tesla AI-chip supply agreement. CHIPS Act funding was reduced from $6.4B to $4.7B as total investment was scaled from $44B to $37B.
cre: Advanced-node foundry on a 1,200-acre campus in Williamson County. Equipment move-in milestone signals transition from shell-and-core construction to operational ramp, triggering renewed supplier-ecosystem site searches throughout the Taylor/Hutto/Georgetown corridor. Second fab review adds potential for further campus expansion.
changeNote: Two changes since prior tracking: (1) Equipment move-in ceremony completed April 2026, marking transition from construction to ramp; (2) Samsung announced early-stage review of Taylor Fab 2 focused on 2nm, with expansion driven by Tesla AI-chip orders. CHIPS award reduced from $6.4B to $4.7B and total capex revised down from $44B to $37B.

site: Terafab – Grimes County Site (Gibbons Creek Reservoir)
sum: SpaceX filed a property tax abatement application with Grimes County on May 6, 2026 for a vertically integrated semiconductor manufacturing and advanced computing facility at the former Gibbons Creek Reservoir coal-plant site, roughly 90 miles northeast of Austin. Phase-1 capex is estimated at $55B with total potential investment of up to $119B across all phases. The county commissioners court scheduled a public hearing for June 3, 2026. Intel joined the project in April 2026 to contribute manufacturing expertise including its 14A process node. A separate ~$3B Tesla prototype fab is being built concurrently at Gigafactory Texas in Austin.
cre: Greenfield mega-campus proposal on a large former industrial site in rural Grimes County. If approved, would be one of the largest single industrial real estate commitments in U.S. history, anchoring a new supplier and infrastructure corridor between Bryan-College Station and Houston. Public hearing June 3 is the next formal approval gate.
changeNote: New project — no prior tracking entry. First formal site commitment: SpaceX tax abatement filing in Grimes County dated May 6, 2026. Intel partnership formalized April 7, 2026. Announced capex far exceeds the $20B figure cited at the March 2026 announcement.

site: Advanced Technology Fabrication (Prototype Fab) – Gigafactory Texas
sum: Tesla is constructing a ~$3B prototype semiconductor fabrication facility on its existing Gigafactory Texas campus in Austin. Active construction was confirmed by aerial observation as of May 8, 2026, including earthwork, material staging, and road extension work on the East site. Elon Musk described the pilot line as capable of a few thousand wafer starts per month and designed to test chip-manufacturing ideas ahead of the full-scale Terafab in Grimes County. Small-batch production of Tesla's AI5 chip is targeted for 2026, with volume production in 2027.
cre: In-campus fab addition within Tesla's existing Gigafactory Texas footprint. Represents incremental industrial real estate demand for specialty construction trades, ultra-pure utilities, and process-chemical supply in the Austin metro, and will drive ancillary supplier-space needs near the existing campus.
changeNote: New project — no prior tracking entry. Active construction confirmed May 8, 2026. Elon Musk disclosed on Tesla Q1 2026 earnings call that Tesla is building this prototype fab separate from the full-scale Grimes County Terafab.

site: John Palmour Manufacturing Center for Silicon Carbide (The JP)
sum: Wolfspeed emerged from Chapter 11 bankruptcy in September 2025 and subsequently received $698.6M in IRS tax refunds under the CHIPS Act Section 48D Advanced Manufacturing Investment Credit, boosting its cash balance to ~$1.5B. The Siler City facility achieved production readiness in late 2025 and is currently growing its first batches of 200mm silicon carbide crystal boules, with initial wafer shipments to Wolfspeed's Mohawk Valley fab already underway. The $750M CHIPS Act PMT signed in October 2024 had not been formally awarded as of late 2025, as the Trump administration was renegotiating terms.
cre: Two-million-square-foot SiC crystal-growth and wafer manufacturing plant on a 445-acre site in Chatham County. Post-bankruptcy operational restart confirms the facility is active, making it a live anchor for supplier, chemical, and logistics real estate in the Siler City area. Room for an additional 2M SF of expansion on site.
changeNote: Multiple changes: (1) Wolfspeed exited Chapter 11 bankruptcy September 2025; (2) Received $698.6M IRS Section 48D tax refund December 2025; (3) Siler City fab confirmed operationally active — first 200mm SiC crystal boules in production and initial wafers shipping to NY fab. CHIPS Act $750M grant status remains unresolved under Trump administration review.

sum: The Department of Commerce signed a preliminary memorandum of terms (PMT) with Absolics, an SKC subsidiary, for CHIPS Act funding to support construction of a 120,000 SF glass substrate manufacturing facility in Covington, Georgia. The facility will produce glass core substrates used in advanced semiconductor packaging for AI, high-performance computing, and data center chips. The project is estimated to support over 1,000 construction jobs and approximately 200 permanent manufacturing and R&D jobs, and is linked to a technology partnership with Georgia Tech.
cre: Greenfield advanced-packaging materials plant in Newton County, Georgia. Glass substrate supply is a critical node in the AI chip supply chain, and this facility's CHIPS PMT signals a high probability of construction moving forward. Generates demand for industrial land, specialty construction, and chemical-handling infrastructure near the I-20 corridor east of Atlanta.
changeNote: New project surfaced — not in prior known list. CHIPS Act PMT documented on SIA supply-chain investment tracker. Capex figure not publicly disclosed; SF (120,000) and job figures from SIA description.
